Object.Name: PlatformMonitoring
Object.Description1:
This LwM2M Object provides platform-scoped monitoring state for GEISA-capable platform functions, components, services, queues, APIs, and operational paths.
Each Object Instance represents the platform's monitoring view for one platform component or for an aggregate platform scope. Values may be produced from bounded read-time checks, recently cached local checks, platform status snapshots, or retained component telemetry. This Object is intended for EMA health, LwM2M client health, registration and bootstrap state, upstream reporting health, local API broker or service health, app message bridge health, local monitoring function health, event log service health, and upstream queue or spool health. Platform Implementors may have their own components mapped into this instance via the use of the vendor-specific reservation IDs.
This Object does not require continuous telemetry collection. It does not imply a daemon, service, process, or engine architecture. Optional resources may be unavailable when the platform does not implement, expose, or retain the corresponding function, counter, timestamp, or component state.

Item.Name: Component Type
Item.Description:
Type of GEISA Platform component represented by this Object Instance.
0 = Unknown 1 = Aggregate GEISA Platform 2 = Edge Management Agent 3 = LwM2M client 4 = GEISA API broker 5 = GEISA API service or function 6 = App message bridge 7 = Local monitoring function 8 = Event log service 9 = Upstream queue or spool service 10 = Security or credential service 11..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined component types 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific component types

Item.Name: Component Name
Item.Description: Human-readable component name assigned by the platform. This value is intended for display, diagnostics, and log correlation.

Item.Name: Component Instance ID
Item.Description: Platform-local identifier for the component instance represented by this Object Instance. This value is intended for correlation with logs, events, and platform diagnostics.

Item.Name: Component Version
Item.Description: Version of the GEISA Platform component, when available from runtime metadata, service metadata, manifest or package metadata, or platform-generated records.

Item.Name: Component Health State
Item.Description:
Current health state for this GEISA Platform component.
0 = Unknown 1 = Healthy 2 = Degraded 3 = Failed 4 = Disabled 5 = Not reporting 6 = Starting 7 = Stopping 8..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined health states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific health states

Item.Name: Health Reason
Item.Description: Short platform-generated reason associated with the current Component Health State.

Item.Name: Last Health Transition Time
Item.Description: Time at which the Component Health State most recently changed. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Component Start Time
Item.Description: Time at which the current or most recent execution of the component started, when available from the platform. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Restart Count Current Period
Item.Description: Number of component restarts during the current monitoring period.

Item.Name: Monitoring Window
Item.Description: Monitoring window in seconds used for current-period counters and derived health evaluation for this component.

Item.Name: Monitoring Period Start Time
Item.Description: Start time for the active monitoring period used by current-period counters and derived health state. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Update Timestamp
Item.Description: Time at which the platform most recently refreshed, generated, or updated the monitoring snapshot represented by this object instance. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: LwM2M Registration State
Item.Description:
Current LwM2M registration state, when this Object Instance represents an EMA, LwM2M client, or aggregate GEISA Platform scope.
0 = Unknown 1 = Not registered 2 = Registering 3 = Registered 4 = Registration update pending 5 = Registration failed 6 = Registration expired 7 = Deregistering 8..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined registration states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific registration states

Item.Name: Last Registration Time
Item.Description: Time of the most recent successful LwM2M registration, when known. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Registration Update Time
Item.Description: Time of the most recent successful LwM2M registration update, when known. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Registration Lifetime
Item.Description: Current LwM2M registration lifetime in seconds.

Item.Name: Bootstrap State
Item.Description:
Current bootstrap state, when applicable.
0 = Unknown 1 = Not required 2 = Required 3 = In progress 4 = Completed 5 = Failed 6..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined bootstrap states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific bootstrap states

Item.Name: Upstream Communication State
Item.Description:
Current upstream communication state for GEISA Platform reporting.
0 = Unknown 1 = Disabled 2 = Enabled down 3 = Enabled up 4 = Degraded 5 = Backlogged 6 = Blocked by policy 7..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined upstream communication states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific upstream communication states

Item.Name: Last Upstream Success Time
Item.Description: Timestamp of the most recent successful upstream send, handoff, delivery, or communication observed or retained by the platform for this component, function, or path, when available. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Last Upstream Error Time
Item.Description: Timestamp of the most recent upstream send, handoff, delivery, or communication error observed or retained by the platform for this component, function, or path, when available. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Upstream Error Count Current Period
Item.Description: Count of upstream send, handoff, delivery, or communication errors observed or retained by the platform during the current monitoring period.

Item.Name: Active Observe Count
Item.Description: Count of active observe relationships for the monitored component, function, or path where such telemetry is available.

Item.Name: Failed Notify Count Current Period
Item.Description: Count of failed Notify deliveries during the current monitoring period where such telemetry is available.

Item.Name: Suppressed Event Count Current Period
Item.Description: Count of events suppressed by policy, throttling, coalescing, backpressure, or other platform behavior during the current monitoring period where such telemetry is available.

Item.Name: Dropped Event Count Current Period
Item.Description: Count of events dropped due to queueing, transport, resource, policy, or delivery failure during the current monitoring period where such telemetry is available.

Item.Name: Queue Health State
Item.Description:
Health state of the upstream queue or spool associated with this component.
0 = Unknown 1 = Healthy 2 = Degraded 3 = Full 4 = Failed 5 = Disabled 6..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined queue health states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific queue health states

Item.Name: Pending Upstream Messages
Item.Description: Number of pending upstream GEISA Platform messages currently queued or spooled for delivery.

Item.Name: Pending Upstream Bytes
Item.Description: Total bytes currently queued or spooled for upstream GEISA Platform delivery.

Item.Name: Oldest Pending Message Time
Item.Description: Timestamp of the oldest pending upstream or platform message in the monitored queue or path when one exists. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Dropped Upstream Messages Current Period
Item.Description: Number of upstream GEISA Platform messages dropped during the current monitoring period.

Item.Name: Expired Upstream Messages Current Period
Item.Description: Number of queued or spooled upstream GEISA Platform messages that expired before successful delivery during the current monitoring period.

Item.Name: API Broker Health State
Item.Description:
Health state of the local GEISA API broker, when applicable.
0 = Unknown 1 = Healthy 2 = Degraded 3 = Failed 4 = Disabled 5..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined API broker health states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific API broker health states

Item.Name: API Request Count Current Period
Item.Description: Count of API requests observed or retained by the monitored API or function during the current monitoring period. Generic event-log activity does not by itself satisfy this resource unless it is also an API request counted by that monitored function.

Item.Name: API Error Count Current Period
Item.Description: Count of API errors observed or retained by the monitored API or function during the current monitoring period.

Item.Name: Unauthorized API Attempt Count Current Period
Item.Description: Count of unauthorized API requests observed or retained by the monitored API or function during the current monitoring period.

Item.Name: App Message Bridge Health State
Item.Description:
Health state of the local app message bridge that connects GEISA API messaging to ADM/LwM2M upstream or downstream handling, when applicable.
0 = Unknown 1 = Healthy 2 = Degraded 3 = Failed 4 = Disabled 5..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined bridge health states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific bridge health states

Item.Name: Event Log Service Health State
Item.Description:
Health state of the local event log service or durable event recording path, when applicable.
0 = Unknown 1 = Healthy 2 = Degraded 3 = Failed 4 = Disabled 5..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined event log health states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific event log health states

Item.Name: Local Monitoring Function Health State
Item.Description:
Health state of the local monitoring function responsible for producing, refreshing, or exposing platform monitoring data when such a function is present. This allows embedded implementations, standalone implementations, disabled profiles, and profiles where no separate local monitoring function exists.
0 = Unknown 1 = Healthy 2 = Degraded 3 = Failed 4 = Disabled 5..127 = Reserved for future GEISA-defined monitoring function health states 128..255 = Reserved for vendor-specific or operator-specific monitoring function health states

Item.Name: Last Durable Event Time
Item.Description: Time at which this component most recently wrote or caused a durable event or log record to be written. The value is encoded as LwM2M Time using Unix epoch seconds. The encoded value does not carry timezone metadata. Platform-local interpretation uses /3/0/15 Timezone, with fallback to /3/0/14 UTC Offset when Timezone is unavailable, unless a more specific ADM or EMS policy applies.

Item.Name: Request Status Refresh
Item.Description: Request that the platform refresh the current status of this platform component and update this Object Instance. Platforms may deny this operation or leave it unsupported according to operator policy, authorization, component type, connectivity state, device state, safety considerations, or implementation profile.

Item.Name: Reset Monitoring Counters
Item.Description: Reset current-period monitoring counters for this Object Instance and start a new monitoring period. Platforms may deny this operation or leave it unsupported according to operator policy, authorization, component type, connectivity state, device state, safety considerations, or implementation profile.

Item.Name: Flush Upstream Queue
Item.Description: Request immediate delivery of queued or spooled upstream messages associated with this component. Platforms may deny this operation or leave it unsupported according to operator policy, authorization, component type, connectivity state, device state, safety considerations, or implementation profile.

Item.Name: Restart Component
Item.Description: Request restart of the component represented by this Object Instance. Platforms may deny this operation or leave it unsupported according to operator policy, authorization, component type, connectivity state, device state, safety considerations, or implementation profile.
